concerns: Plan says '--color-danger for granted' but the color semantics should be verified — granted permissions increase debt (risk), so red/danger is correct UX-wise, but the plan's format-permissions helper description parenthetically says '--color-success for denied/prompt' which inverts normal success semantics. Ensure this inversion is intentional and clearly commented in code so future maintainers understand why denied=success (lower debt).

The plan proposes 'permission-debt-module.ts' as the main file name but existing modules use receipt-oriented naming (fingerprint-receipt.ts). Consider 'permission-debt-receipt.ts' to stay consistent with the established pattern.

Plan step for wiring into main.ts says 'append to #dashboard' but the actual mounting goes through src/layout/shell.ts which creates the dashboard grid. The implementer should check shell.ts to understand the real mounting flow (main.ts calls renderIntro then renderShell) rather than directly appending to #dashboard.

The plan's CSS file is named 'permission-debt.css' but should confirm whether existing modules use CSS Modules (.module.css) or plain scoped CSS — the fingerprint module and intro module use plain CSS with class-prefix scoping, so the plan's approach is fine but should use consistent class prefixes like 'permission-debt-' to avoid leakage.

Clipboard permission ('clipboard-read') is correctly flagged as a risk but the plan doesn't specify which clipboard permission name to query — the permissions-adapter already handles this (check its PERMISSIONS list), so the module should rely on checkPermissions() output rather than hardcoding names.
